seoTitle: How to Enable CSAT Surveys in Zendesk
metaDescription: Turn on Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) surveys in Zendesk to start collecting ratings on your support tickets and measuring agent performance.
intro: Without concrete feedback, you're guessing how satisfied customers actually are with your support. This guide covers activating Zendesk's built-in CSAT survey tools, customizing the rating scale, enabling the right channels, and confirming feedback is flowing into your reports.

steps.heading: Open Business Rules
steps.body: In the Admin Center, go to Objects and Rules and navigate to the Business Rules section.

steps.heading: Find Customer Satisfaction settings
steps.body: Click into the Customer Satisfaction option to access the survey configuration.

steps.heading: Choose your channels
steps.body: Locate the Channels section and select which interfaces, like email or messaging, should trigger surveys.

steps.heading: Activate the survey
steps.body: Click Activate to turn on the default survey triggers for your selected channels.

steps.heading: Customize the rating scale
steps.body: Review the rating scale options and add optional follow-up questions if you want more detailed feedback.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Save your settings globally, then check your dashboard to confirm incoming feedback is displaying correctly.

faq.question: How soon after a ticket closes does the survey go out?
faq.answer: Zendesk typically sends the survey through an automated request that runs on a set delay, commonly around 24 hours, after resolution.

faq.question: Can I customize the survey rating scale?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust between a simple good versus bad option or a more detailed numeric scale.

faq.question: Where do I see the CSAT results once collected?
faq.answer: Feedback appears in your analytics dashboard, which you can review to track agent and team performance over time.

faq.question: Can I send CSAT surveys over messaging channels, not just email?
faq.answer: Yes, you can enable survey triggers across supported channels including messaging tickets in addition to email.
